key_quotes: my daughter got in last spring and we're out of state and just could not afford it... almost $60,000 per year
The city of auburn/the university have allowed an unethical towing monopoly around the campus with unethical towing practices for at least 35 years
In my experience if you push back at all in person they will give you the entire deposit back
key_points: Out-of-state total costs approach $60K yearly, limiting accessibility
Predatory towing companies near campus have plagued students for decades
Landlords illegally withhold deposits, but students who dispute usually win

key_quotes: You should expect to have the time of your life. I reflect back on my time at AU as one of the best decisions I ever made
I'm from 14 hours away and I just feel like I have no friends here and maybe I wasn't meant for the sec
Stay away from Sky Bar
key_points: Football, Greek life, and bars create a vibrant, beloved social scene
Out-of-state and international students often struggle to find social footing
Sky Bar has decades of credible drink-spiking and assault allegations

key_quotes: Grab a lemonade from Toomer's drug store, pizza at the Amsterdam Cafe, Brunswick stew at the best BBQ in the world
2 hot dogs, fries and a drink = $30 there. What fucking college student eats like that
Please god can we get a cheap bar in that space... a student can dream
key_points: On-campus dining is rarely discussed by students
Local alumni favorites include Toomer's lemonade, Amsterdam Cafe, Brunswick stew
Students want more affordable options after downtown closures like Five Guys

key_quotes: Has anyone else ever felt like they just want to transfer or something? I'm really sad and just want to go home. Does it get better?
For ten years, I've carried that trauma silently, battling high-functioning depression, denial, and moments of suicidal idealization
college is really what you make of it. Auburn has truly incredible opportunities but you have to seek them out
key_points: University mental health support is rarely mentioned or praised
At least one student posted publicly about isolation and transfer plans
Students are told college is 'what you make of it' — self-advocacy required

key_quotes: Auburn University gives full tuition with an ACT score of 34
we're out of state and just could not afford it. We didn't qualify for any aid... almost $60,000 per year
the aid I was promised is gone... the school is telling me I have to register it as a 9-credit internship... $14k
key_points: ACT 34+ earns full tuition; Auburn excels for high-achieving in-state students
Out-of-state aid is limited; $50–60K yearly deters many applicants
Athletic scholarship stripped via NIL lawsuit fallout; student then charged $14K

key_quotes: professors have been hit or miss
I hope they are wearing respirators to tear it down. The defining characteristics of Parker has to be black mold
Auburn University requires faculty to review courses to comply with anti-DEI law
key_points: Engineering, architecture, and pre-vet programs are consistently well-regarded
Instruction quality varies sharply by professor across departments
Mandatory anti-DEI curriculum audits are alarming students and faculty deeply

key_quotes: Auburn's administration is caving to fascists. Shameful
AU campus security tried to stop the protest, APD let it happen
when I was [a student], Auburn hired many of its econ professors from the Mises Institute and my classes were basically rants on how evil the Federal Reserve is
key_points: Administration faces criticism for coaching hires, protests, and DEI capitulation
Forced faculty course audits to comply with Alabama anti-DEI law angered campus
Security reportedly shut down peaceful protests while past allowing Spencer to speak

key_quotes: One of the most beautiful campuses I've visited
Everything that made it wonderful from the late 2000s-2015ish is mostly gone. Lots of high-rise, shoddily built 'luxury' housing stands where staples used to be
Just walking around campus, so beautiful. I also enjoyed going out to Chewacla state park
key_points: Campus is widely considered one of the most beautiful in the country
Overdevelopment has replaced authentic spots with luxury high-rises and chains
Chewacla State Park and Tuskegee National Forest offer nearby outdoor escapes

key_quotes: committed to Auburn University for the Class of 2026 with a dual major in Aerospace Engineering and Musical Theater
Employers won't care if you went to UNA or Auburn or UA
I reflect back on my time at AU as one of the best decisions I ever made
key_points: Engineering, aerospace, and vet programs are praised for career preparation
Alumni generally reflect positively on Auburn's career value in retrospect
No concrete job placement rates or salary data surfaces in student discussions

key_quotes: if you're getting a full ride, it's hard to beat, financially speaking
out of state tuition (est. 50k) is WAY too much for me
I had to pay almost as much as I earned the entire summer so that I could graduate
key_points: In-state students with merit aid get exceptional value at Auburn
Out-of-state students paying $60K yearly find better deals elsewhere
Lost scholarships and surprise $14K internship charges signal unreliable financial agreements

key_quotes: Auburn normally has the reputation of being good to visiting fans. Glad that is the experience that you got
I was a conservative when I entered Auburn and left a liberal
i got here in 2021 and let me tell you, it has changed SO much in just that amount of time (not in a good way)
key_points: Auburn has fierce school pride, legendary football culture, and genuine community warmth
Campus leans conservative, reflecting Alabama's political climate broadly
Beloved by alumni, but current students say the town has worsened recently

key_quotes: In 2015, while living in Auburn, I was drugged, kidnapped, and raped after being at Sky Bar... I've carried that trauma silently for ten years
Auburn is the only place in my life I have had the unfortunate personal experience [of antisemitism]
Being on that list alongside Liberty University and Hillsdale College says a lot
key_points: Sky Bar: decades of credible drugging and sexual assault allegations reported publicly
Antisemitism: graffiti on campus and personal accounts from Jewish alumni confirmed
Campus climate: anti-DEI laws enforced, protests suppressed, Pentagon 'non-woke' designation received

key_quotes: One of the best experiences of my life. Congrats to her! It's gonna be awesome [about AUMB]
I plan to do Mechanical Engineering and hopefully join the Formula SAE club
By far and away, people in Auburn were the most friendly and welcoming people we have EVER encountered while traveling to an away game
key_points: Auburn Marching Band offers transformative friendships and deep community belonging
Formula SAE and engineering clubs provide rare hands-on real-world technical experience
Toomer's Corner rolling tradition is a uniquely memorable cultural experience universally praised
